title: Today's AI Brief: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
summary: A look at Google's Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 demos, and Airbnb's new AI lab.
imageAlt: Image from source article for 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageCaption: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageSourceTitle: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action

body.text: I'm checking out some fresh developments in the world of artificial intelligence. Google's making waves with its Gemini models, specifically Omni and 3.5. These models are designed to showcase the ability to reason and create, which could have significant implications for various industries. It's always exciting to see AI advancements, and I'm curious to see how these models will be used.

Google has released 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action. These demos provide a glimpse into the capabilities of these models, showcasing their potential applications. Gemini Omni seems to combine reasoning and creativity, while Gemini 3.5 is built to assist with various tasks. I'm intrigued by the possibilities, especially in areas like content creation and problem-solving.

In other news, Airbnb's CEO, Brian Chesky, plans to launch a new AI lab. This move suggests that Airbnb is looking to leverage AI to enhance its services or create new ones. I'm curious to see how Airbnb will use AI to improve the user experience, maybe with personalized recommendations or smarter booking systems. It's interesting that Chesky wants to explore AI's potential, especially considering Airbnb's existing platform and user base.

Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab is also worth a quick mention.
